name: Beginner Rescue Prompts
description: Short rescue prompts for people who get stuck, feel confused, get weak answers, or do not know what to ask AI next.
author: SayNext contributors

prompts.title: I want to
prompts.text: I want to... Help me clarify the request first, then ask what information you still need.

prompts.title: Ask me first
prompts.text: Ask me up to 3 necessary questions first, then help me do it.

prompts.title: Clarify the task
prompts.text: Turn this idea into a clear task with a goal, deliverable, and first step.

prompts.title: Ask if unclear
prompts.text: If my request is unclear, ask follow-up questions first instead of guessing what I mean.

prompts.title: Goals and limits
prompts.text: Help me clarify the goal, constraints, available resources, and success criteria first.

prompts.title: Needed info
prompts.text: Tell me what information I should provide first so you can help more accurately.

prompts.title: Rewrite for AI
prompts.text: Rewrite my request into a clear, executable instruction that is suitable for an AI.

prompts.title: Plain language
prompts.text: Explain this in plain language so someone without background knowledge can understand it.

prompts.title: Simpler
prompts.text: I really do not understand. Explain it again in a simpler way, starting with the key point I should remember.

prompts.title: Example
prompts.text: Use a simple everyday example, then connect it back to the original concept.

prompts.title: Step by step
prompts.text: Teach me step by step. For each step, explain the purpose before the action.

prompts.title: Main point first
prompts.text: Give me the main point in 3 sentences first, then add only the necessary details.

prompts.title: Beginner level
prompts.text: Explain this for a complete beginner. Do not assume I already know the background.

prompts.title: No jargon
prompts.text: Replace technical terms with plain words. If a term is necessary, explain it first.

prompts.title: What do you recommend
prompts.text: Explain why you made that judgment, then give me your most recommended approach.

prompts.title: Rethink
prompts.text: This answer is not good enough. Recheck the problem, think from another angle, and answer again.

prompts.title: Three options
prompts.text: Give me 3 different approaches and compare their pros and cons.

prompts.title: Explain reasons
prompts.text: Do not just give the conclusion. Explain the reasoning behind it.

prompts.title: Check answer
prompts.text: Check whether your previous answer has errors, missing pieces, or unreasonable assumptions.

prompts.title: More practical
prompts.text: Give me a more practical and actionable answer, and tell me the first step to take.

prompts.title: Higher standard
prompts.text: Answer again using a more professional standard, and include the quality points you would check.

prompts.title: Find a way
prompts.text: Explain why you cannot do it, then suggest a safe, practical alternative that gets close to my goal.

prompts.title: Alternative
prompts.text: If you cannot do it directly, suggest the closest practical alternative.

prompts.title: Small steps
prompts.text: Break this into smaller steps that are still possible to do now.

prompts.title: Explain limits
prompts.text: Tell me what the limitation is and how to reduce its impact.

prompts.title: Do the possible part
prompts.text: If you cannot do the full version, do the parts that are possible.

prompts.title: Safe alternatives
prompts.text: Suggest at least 3 legal, safe, and policy-compliant alternatives.

prompts.title: What can you help with
prompts.text: First explain which parts you can still help with, then explain what you cannot help with and why.

prompts.title: Expert views
prompts.text: Find how professionals or credible sources approach this, what they think, and summarize the key points.

prompts.title: Latest sources
prompts.text: Find up-to-date information, include source links, and note the dates of the information.

prompts.title: Compare views
prompts.text: Compare different viewpoints. Do not give me only one answer.

prompts.title: Facts vs inference
prompts.text: Mark which parts are facts and which parts are your inference.

prompts.title: Mainstream and minority
prompts.text: Summarize the mainstream approach and any credible minority opinions.

prompts.title: Reliable sources
prompts.text: Prioritize official docs, research, professional organizations, or primary sources instead of general web articles.

prompts.title: Summary and action
prompts.text: Turn the research into key takeaways, risk notes, and actionable recommendations.

prompts.title: Best plan
prompts.text: Create a clear, executable best plan. For important decisions, confirm with me using multiple choice questions.

prompts.title: Goals and risks
prompts.text: List the goal, constraints, risks, and steps first.

prompts.title: Timeframes
prompts.text: Split the plan into what I can do today, this week, and later.

prompts.title: Improve plan
prompts.text: Review and improve this plan by adding missing steps, risks, and checkpoints.

prompts.title: Milestones
prompts.text: Break this into clear milestones.

prompts.title: Likely failure
prompts.text: Point out where this plan is most likely to fail.

prompts.title: Prioritize
prompts.text: Help me prioritize what to do first.

prompts.title: Start
prompts.text: Start executing based on the current plan. If you hit an important decision, ask me using multiple choice.

prompts.title: First version
prompts.text: Create a first version that can be reviewed. Do not aim for perfection yet.

prompts.title: One step
prompts.text: Do one step at a time. After each step, ask me whether to continue.

prompts.title: Use the plan
prompts.text: Start producing the result based on the plan above.

prompts.title: Usable version
prompts.text: Give me a version I can use directly, not just general direction or principles.

prompts.title: Copyable format
prompts.text: Format the result so I can copy and use it directly.

prompts.title: Do it
prompts.text: Do not only give advice. Help me complete it directly.

prompts.title: Recommend next steps
prompts.text: Based on the current state, suggest the 3 most valuable next steps and explain the priority order.

prompts.title: Pick one
prompts.text: Help me decide the single best next step and explain why.

prompts.title: Small action
prompts.text: Turn the next step into one action I can finish within 10 minutes.

prompts.title: Ask first
prompts.text: If the next step is not clear yet, ask me up to 3 key questions first.

prompts.title: Check risks
prompts.text: Before continuing, point out the most likely risks in the next step.

prompts.title: Checkpoint
prompts.text: Set the next checkpoint: what must be done before we continue.

prompts.title: Move forward
prompts.text: Move forward with the next step directly. After finishing, tell me what you did and what is still missing.

prompts.title: Full review
prompts.text: Review the current result thoroughly, find problems, risks, and missing pieces, then recommend the next improvements.

prompts.title: Review and improve
prompts.text: After finishing, check for problems, fix weak spots, and improve the result until it is more usable.

prompts.title: Professional review
prompts.text: Review this result using a professional standard.

prompts.title: Find likely errors
prompts.text: Point out the most likely problems and help me fix them.

prompts.title: Missing or unclear
prompts.text: Check for anything missing, contradictory, or unclear.

prompts.title: User view
prompts.text: Review this from the user's point of view.

prompts.title: Simplify
prompts.text: Give me a shorter and easier-to-understand version.

prompts.title: Ready to deliver
prompts.text: Improve this until it is ready to deliver, and list anything I still need to confirm.
